diagrams.description: Political Theory is the normative and conceptual study of political ideas and ideals. This flowchart illustrates its analytical logic: the ultimate goal is to understand, critique, and construct political norms and institutions. It integrates major traditions of thought (classical, liberal, conservative, socialist, feminist, postcolonial) with foundational concepts (justice, liberty, equality, authority, democracy, rights). These are examined through specific methods (normative analysis, conceptual analysis, textual interpretation, critical theory) that generate evaluative frameworks. The resulting insights inform both the critique of existing political arrangements and the design of more just political practices and institutions.
